# game4.html Stage 4a push contact-model parameter calibration sweep

> Read-only research tool output. Does not modify game4.html or return-studio.html. Recommendation only — whether/how to apply is a follow-up decision.
Updated: 2026-07-14T02:48:17.075Z
Presets: 11 (11-preset calibration set, same exclusions as push-sustainable-rally-sweep-calibration.js: no_spin_long_forehand, no_spin_long_backhand, backspin_short_forehand_2, backspin_short_backhand_2, backspin_short_backhand)
maxRounds per rally: 12. Descriptive backspin label threshold on canonical outgoing topspin: <= -15 (label only, not a survival gate).

## Design principle under test

Commit 221d4d3 ported return-studio.html's Stage 4a spring-damper substepped push contact model (architecture only) into game4.html, replacing the old instantaneous-collision bounceOffPlane for push. Re-running game4-push-sustainable-rally-validation.js afterward showed rally survival got WORSE (avgRounds 3.364->0.909) even though backspin-preservation got better (backspinFraction 8.11%->40%). This tool searches the contact-model PARAMETERS (TANGENT_KP, PADDLE_BLEND, PADDLE_SPRING_K's derivation half-period, PADDLE_RESTITUTION_LOW/HIGH) for a combo that improves rally survival without wrecking single-shot landing, scoring both objectives separately (not collapsed into one scalar) since this may be — and turned out to be, see below — a genuine Pareto tradeoff.

## Ranking formula (stated explicitly for audit)

1. DISQUALIFY any combo with singleShot.okCount < 6 (floor = baseline's singleShot.okCount [7] minus 1 — allows losing at most one preset off baseline's single-shot landing rate). Disqualified combos sort to the bottom regardless of rally numbers.
2. Among qualified combos: maximize avgRounds (rally survival, the headline objective).
3. Tie-break: maximize backspinFraction (spin-preservation — the whole point of the Stage 4a port).
4. Tie-break: maximize minRounds (worst-case preset).
5. Tie-break: maximize singleShot.okCount.

## Grid dimensions (staged: coarse -> restitution -> refine)

- Stage 1: TANGENT_KP x PADDLE_BLEND x halfPeriodMs = 4 x 4 x 4 = 64 combos x 11 presets x up to 12 rounds each (+ 11-preset single-shot check per combo). PADDLE_RESTITUTION_LOW/HIGH held at deployed values (0.9/0.75).
  TANGENT_KP candidates: 0.3, 0.6, 1, 1.8 (deployed: 1)
  PADDLE_BLEND candidates: 0, 0.3, 0.65, 1 (deployed: 0.65; 0=rigid contact normal, 1=fully ball-approach-direction)
  halfPeriodMs candidates: 3, 5, 6.5, 8 (deployed: 5ms; PADDLE_SPRING_K = BALL_MASS * (PI/halfPeriod)^2)
- Stage 2: PADDLE_RESTITUTION_LOW x PADDLE_RESTITUTION_HIGH (HIGH<=LOW only) = up to 4x4, actually run 16 combos, TANGENT_KP/PADDLE_BLEND/halfPeriodMs fixed at stage-1 winner (TANGENT_KP=0.3 PADDLE_BLEND=0.3 halfPeriodMs=8 PADDLE_RESTITUTION_LOW=- PADDLE_RESTITUTION_HIGH=-). Attack-technique single-shot landing (forehand_attack+backhand_attack x 11 presets = 22 shots) checked per candidate as a regression signal (dynamicPaddleEpsilon, used by the attack contact path, also reads these two constants).
  PADDLE_RESTITUTION_LOW candidates: 0.8, 0.85, 0.9, 0.95 (deployed: 0.9)
  PADDLE_RESTITUTION_HIGH candidates: 0.65, 0.7, 0.75, 0.8 (deployed: 0.75)
- Stage 3 (refine): TANGENT_KP x PADDLE_BLEND x halfPeriodMs narrow grid around the better of stage1/stage2 winners = 3 x 3 x 3 = 27 combos, restitution fixed at 0.85/0.65.
  TANGENT_KP candidates: 0.1, 0.3, 0.5
  PADDLE_BLEND candidates: 0.15, 0.3, 0.45
  halfPeriodMs candidates: 7.5, 8, 8.5

failReason counts: {"掛網":7,"出界":4}

If the top-5-by-avgRounds and top-5-by-singleShot tables above do not overlap, that is direct evidence of a genuine Pareto tradeoff between rally survival and single-shot landing under this parameter family — see interpretation note below.

## Interpretation note

The best-by-avgRounds and best-by-singleShot candidate sets overlap — the overall final winner above is not a compromise sacrificing one objective for the other.
